test.name: FamilySearch API search should not trigger search-external-sites
test.description: User asks to search FamilySearch. FamilySearch is queried via the MCP API tools (record_search, fulltext_search) — that's search-records, not search-external-sites. search-external-sites targets sites without API access (Ancestry, MyHeritage, FindMyPast) where the user manually clicks and captures results.

input.user_message: Search FamilySearch for Patrick Flynn in the 1850 census.

negative.explanation: FamilySearch is accessible via the MCP record_search tool — searching it doesn't require the click-capture-analyze workflow that search-external-sites is designed for. search-external-sites' purpose is bridging the gap to commercial sites (Ancestry, MyHeritage) where automated API search isn't available; FamilySearch has API access.

judge_context: Should route to search-records rather than generate a FamilySearch URL for manual click-through
